subject: Databases
title: Composite Indexes
description: A quick win: understand Composite Indexes.

hook.text: A query filters by country AND city. A single-column index on country helps, but a composite index is better.

buildup.text: A composite index on (country, city) sorts by country first, then city within each country.

discovery.text: The leftmost prefix rule: (country, city) supports queries on country alone, but NOT city alone.

twist.text: Column order matters enormously. (city, country) and (country, city) serve different queries.

climax.text: Design composite indexes around your most common query patterns. Order columns by selectivity.

punchline.text: Composite indexes: column order defines which queries they accelerate.

quiz.question: What is the leftmost prefix rule for composite indexes?
options: The index can only be used for queries that start with the leftmost column in the index
The first column in the index must always be a string
You must always query all columns in the composite index
